_readme: g2-import-v2 -> v2.2 rubric-iteration assumption block ASM-1820..1828 — EMITTED by the Fable BUILD agent (2026-07-12) for central registration by the coordinator at successor-record freeze; registry/assumptions.jsonl is NOT touched by this build (same discipline as the 1800..1813 block).
Range 1820-1829 verified free against registry/assumptions.jsonl at emission time (max registered id 1813); ids 1820-1828 used.

Scope: the ONE bounded proxy-instrument repair recommended by docs/next/analysis/correctness-track-instrument-assessment.md (sections 2.1 and 4 item 2) before conceding g2 adoption authority to the two-human panel. Design record: docs/next/design/g2-import-v22-rubric-iteration.md. $0 build: mock-only, zero judge calls, NO freeze, NO git actions, NO run.

Rider carried by every forward-looking row, verbatim: PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; same 84 self-authored kernel-v0 slots; point-estimate engineering gate, not statistical superiority; soft non-binding typing only — never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.

rows.claim: g2-import-v2 two-pilot disagreement structure (pilot-20260712-ac1 AC1_A3 0.6909 table 29/2/6/3; real-20260712-auth AC1_A3 0.6222 table 28/1/7/4, kappa 0.0000, AC1 = its own independence ceiling to machine precision): the disagreement core is a FIXED 9-item set persisting in both pilots — pA-yes/pB-no on g2:pi:016,021,025,033,064,089 (6 items); pA-no/pB-yes on g2:pi:036,037,070 (3 items). judge-pB flipped 0/40 items between pilots; judge-pA flipped 2/40 (g2:pi:068 no->yes, g2:pi:071 yes->no), and those two pA flips are exactly the 31/40 -> 29/40 agreement drop that moved AC1 across the 0.65 gate. At pilot-#2 marginals AC1 >= 0.65 <=> >= 30/40 agreements; one converted disagreement passes.
rows.rationale: Item-level diff of the four committed pilot response files; makes PROPOSED-ASM-1690's located channel item-precise and shows the gate-straddle is pA noise atop a stable one-sided pB-strict core.

rows.claim: The pB hedge-strictness channel localises to MULTI-CLAIM hedged composites, not to hedges as such: both judges are 12/12 semantically correct on the repaired simple-hedge calibration set in both pilots (including the world-typicality anchor 'Normally a chair has legs'), while all 9 core-disagreement items carry hedged sentences with linked details (crude census over the 40 pilot items: agreed-yes items average 0.96 linked details per hedged sentence, n=28; disagreement items ~1.7, n=11). The signature features are multi-claim bundles, tacked-on contrasts ('— not before'), elliptical hedged fragments ('Normally a person speaking or writing.'), and embedded second-order qualifiers ('often'); the 3-item pA-no/pB-yes mirror set carries concessive widenings ('or at least', 'But Y can also be'), showing the ambiguity cuts BOTH directions.
rows.rationale: Discriminates the instrument ambiguity (hedge scope over compound sentences, under-determined by the v2 rubric text) from a blanket pB hedge defect; grounds the symmetric repair.

rows.claim: v2.2 rubric refinement (prompt-template-v2.2.txt sha 60d3403721135410b51177545e637f8c12e44e5f18f4ccf8c2ade867dc4cfde1): the v2 template is byte-carried except ONE added bullet — 'A hedged sentence may link several details together — with dashes, commas, "or", "but", or a tacked-on contrast (like "— not before"). The hedge covers the WHOLE sentence: judge it as ONE picture of the usual case, true if that picture, taken as a whole, is right about the usual run of cases, and false if the picture as a whole misdescribes the usual case. Do NOT split a hedged sentence into parts and demand that each detail hold separately or hold always; and do NOT read any part of a hedged sentence (including a tacked-on contrast) as an unhedged ALL-cases claim. A hedged sentence written without its own subject (for example "Normally with a key.") continues the topic of the sentence before it, still at hedged strength.' — plus the mirroring clause in the no-instruction ('and never merely because one linked detail inside a hedged sentence is not, on its own, the usual case, when the sentence's whole picture of the usual case is right'). Unhedged sentences keep the ALL-normal-cases standard verbatim; the change is a no-op on the hedge-free baseline/A1.
rows.rationale: Sentence adverbs take clausal scope in ordinary English; the clarification fixes the rubric to what the sentences already meant. Symmetric: the same whole-sentence standard blocks per-part splitting (the 6-item pB channel) AND keeps whole-picture falsity fully in force (the 3-item mirror channel; cal:hedge-8 anchor).

rows.claim: v2.2 calibration extension (calibration-hedge-v22.jsonl sha 63c247a9e7e7edc8769739753ef3b4c33b502f1b93dd01e12e6a948a4f97f57f): the 6 repaired v2 hedge-calibration items byte-carried + 2 new COMPOSITE-hedge known-answer anchors exercising the new bullet at the operating point, one per failure direction — cal:hedge-7 (umbrella, expected yes: whole picture right though linked details vary; elliptical + dashes + tacked-on contrast) and cal:hedge-8 (bicycle 'Normally ridden indoors', expected no: whole picture misdescribes the usual case though some linked details are true — guards against gestalt-leniency). Cal gates become 10/10 per judge at preflight (2 v1 + 8 hedge) and 16/16 across judges at the pilot. Hedge-flip probe families untouched: the whole-sentence reading rejects their absurd flips as decisively as the per-part reading.
rows.rationale: The exercised-not-argued lesson (five instrument failures at untested interfaces): the new rubric line gets prevalence-free known-answer coverage in BOTH directions before any real item is scored.

rows.claim: MCP-leak calibration artifact: pilot-20260712-ac1 failed its 12/12 cal gate (11/12) solely because a claude.ai MCP connector nondeterministically attached to 1/62 pB headless sessions despite --tools '' --setting-sources '', tripping the fail-closed tools==[] contract on a semantically correct answer (cal:hedge-6, raw '{"answer": "no"}'; 5/5 diagnostic repeats clean). Two-part repair implemented in commit bf75ff84 and NOW documented (closing the dangling 'section 11.8' reference): (i) --strict-mcp-config on every headless claude invocation, validator unchanged; (ii) calibration retry symmetry — the cal block goes through the same process_item retry ladder as real/probe items (it was the only single-attempt channel, so one 1/62-rate process artifact could fail the gate); content contract unchanged (first valid answer final). PIN-DRIFT DISCLOSURE: the frozen record pins run-ontg2v2.py ce2ab5a6... but sanctioned pilot #2 ran the hardened cd8e2f89... (committed 13:56Z, post-freeze 13:20:02Z) with no ops amendment — the sanctioned pilot ran off-pin.
rows.rationale: The infra artifact and the pin drift must be on the record before any successor freeze; the drift is an independent reason the frozen record cannot be re-entered by amendment.

rows.claim: PRE-COMMITMENT (recorded before any v2.2 judge call; to be carried as a verdict-bearing clause of the successor record): if the v2.2 Stage-P pilot's Gwet AC1_A3 on the 40 pinned items is < 0.65 — a SECOND sanctioned AC1 pilot failure for this record family — the GPT-5.6-Sol + Haiku-4.5 proxy pair is RETIRED as an adoption-arm (A3) instrument for the g2-import family: no further proxy-pair rubric iteration may be funded for A3, and the adoption decision passes to the two-human adjudicated panel on the 84 slots — the envelope's sole authority for permanent adoption, under which the v1 primary signal holds on every label bracket including pair-concordant 42/84 >= 34/84. Retirement binds the proxy pair's adoption-arm authority only: no conclusion about H-SOFT/H-SRC/H-SEP in either direction, no retirement for unhedged arms or other families, no binding of the human panel. A PASS licenses exactly the pre-registered ~$5-6 full 796-call run under the successor record, nothing else. Rider verbatim: PROVISIONAL-ON-LLM-PROXY; same 84 self-authored kernel-v0 slots; point-estimate engineering gate, not statistical superiority; soft non-binding typing only — never hard laws; no feasibility conclusion.
rows.rationale: Implements the instrument assessment's closeability call ('fund exactly one v2.2 iteration... with a pre-commitment that a second AC1 failure retires the proxy-pair instrument'); either branch terminates the proxy iteration loop and protects against rubric-iteration shopping.

rows.claim: Mechanism recommendation: v2.2 requires a SUCCESSOR record (registry/experiments/g2-import-v2.2.json, kot-reg/2), NOT a pre-final amendment of the frozen g2-import-v2. Grounds: (i) the rubric is constitutive, not ops — frozen_sha256 covers pins.artifact_hashes including prompt-template-v2.txt, and the only amendment channel the record sanctions is the harness_manifest banner fill; (ii) the Stage-P gate has FIRED — the sanctioned pilot mechanically returned INSTRUMENT-INVALID (pilot AC1 channel) with a pilot-only result assembled, and re-entering Stage-P under the same id would put two authoritative pilot outcomes under one frozen record (a gate-shopping surface); (iii) the record's own verdict-name mapping prescribes 'repair and mint a new frozen run', and design section 11.7's disjunction resolves to 'a new record after' freeze; (iv) v1 -> v2 precedent (same estimand, new record). The successor inherits the estimand identity chain and ALL v2 gates verbatim (pilot AC1_A3 >= 0.65 on the SAME 40 pinned items, decisive >= 36/40, hedge-flip <= 2/8, full-run gates), quarantines both prior pilots as instrument evidence, and pins the v2.2 instrument files + harness. NOT frozen by this build; coordinator action.
rows.rationale: Preserves prereg discipline while enabling the one bounded repair; both standing off-record facts (pin drift, section-11.8 gap) are lawfully absorbed at the successor freeze instead of compounding.

rows.claim: v2.2 call/cost arithmetic: preflight (2 v1-cal + 8 hedge-cal) x 2 = 20; Stage-P pilot (8 cal + 40 real + 8 flip) x 2 = 112; pilot stage total 132 calls ~= $1.06 at the v1-measured <= $0.008/call, <= $1.58 at the pinned conservative $0.012 bound (the ~$1.4 shape preserved). Full-campaign ceiling rises 788 -> 796 (+4 preflight cal + 4 pilot cal); bound-implied worst case 796 x $0.012 = $9.552 <= the $10 hard stop, enforced by the unchanged live dollar/call abort. Pilot operating characteristics unchanged (same 40 items, same AC1 gate, pilot-manifest.json OC block carries over). DISCLOSED RISK (v1->v2 lesson): the whole-sentence reading may shift prevalence further yes-ward from pi ~ 0.84, where every pair coefficient discriminates weakly; the burden-shifted known-answer channels remain load-bearing on an equal footing with AC1, and ASM-1825 bounds the family to this ONE further iteration.
rows.rationale: Sizes the tripwire and the ceiling before the coordinator commits the ~$1.4; licenses nothing.

rows.claim: v2.2 build pass 2026-07-12, $0 (mock only, zero judge calls): run-ontg2v2.py extended with the flag-guarded --rubric v22 path (v22 pins added to the fail-closed pin set; PILOT_CAL_MIN 12 -> 16 and BUDGET_MAX_CALLS 788 -> 796 under the flag only; rubric recorded in preflight-status/pilot-status; rubric-mix guards abort any run mixing v2 and v22 phases), new sha efe134ad0a51c9b49d2ea93d1f2e3341cfcd6a945cfcf492bd9a7e621d1d18e9 — to be pinned by the successor record with both judge CLI banners at its ops amendment. Validation: all five mock verdict paths GREEN under --rubric v22 (go PASS / nogo FAIL / instrument INSTRUMENT-INVALID / pilotfail INSTRUMENT-INVALID with pilot_valid=false / breadth FAIL with breadth_confound=true; runs/mockcheck-v22-20260712, pilot-status rubric=v22, cal 16/16); default-path regression GREEN with rubric=v2 and cal 12/12 (runs/mockcheck-v2default-20260712); rubric-mix guard exercised directly (ERR_ONTG2V2_RUBRIC abort); verify_pins green over the v22 pin set. All frozen v2 files byte-identical: prompt-template-v2.txt 6ba9a6f4..., calibration-hedge.jsonl f8d4e03b..., pilot-manifest.json e4ac7c95..., design doc 5829fad9....
rows.rationale: Green-mock-before-freeze discipline; records exactly what changed on disk and that nothing frozen moved.
